channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitics
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news and analysis from trusted sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸⚔️ Iran Attacks US Bases in Jordan and Bahrain
item.description: Iran targeted American military installations in Jordan and Bahrain, the latest escalation in tit-for-tat strikes with the United States. Tehran warned Gulf neighbors they could face consequences if they allowed US operations from their territory.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg

item.title: 🇺🇳⚔️ UN Chief Warns of Return to Full-Scale War in Middle East
item.description: Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned of the risk of returning to full war in the Middle East after Iran and the United States traded strikes. The UN called for immediate de-escalation and a return to diplomatic negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Asharq Al-Awsat

item.title: 🇺🇸💰📈 US Consumer Inflation Hits Three-Year High Amid Iran War
item.description: Consumer prices rose 4.2% annually in May, the fastest pace in three years, driven by surging energy costs from the Middle East conflict. The data gives the Federal Reserve reason to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺🇰🇷💻 EU and South Korea Sign Digital Trade Pact
item.description: The European Union and South Korea signed a digital trade agreement to facilitate digital transactions and strengthen economic ties. The pact comes as both sides seek to diversify trade relationships amid global uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: US News

item.title: 🌏📉 Foreign Outflows Surge from Asian Equities on Middle East Risks
item.description: Asian equity markets are experiencing significant foreign outflows in June as investors retreat from risk amid escalating Middle East hostilities and a pullback in AI-linked technology stocks. The trend highlights regional vulnerability to geopolitical shocks.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇯🇵💹 Bank of Japan Set to Raise Key Rate to 1.0% in June
item.description: The BOJ will raise its benchmark interest rate this month and again in Q4, taking borrowing costs to 1.25% by year-end as it grows more wary of inflation risks. The yen strengthened on the prospect of tighter policy.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱💥 Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. The Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the border region.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇲🇲🇺🇸 US Diplomat Found Dead in Myanmar, Thai Woman in Custody
item.description: A US State Department official was found dead in Yangon with a Thai woman in custody. The department confirmed the fatality but declined to provide additional details about the circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇵🇰💔 All Aboard Military Helicopter Killed in Pakistan Kashmir Crash
item.description: A Pakistani military helicopter crashed near Muzaffarabad in Pakistani Kashmir, killing all personnel on board. The military did not specify the number of casualties in the incident.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 NATO Conducts Drone Flights Near Ukraine Border
item.description: NATO intelligence drones have been conducting flights near the Ukraine border from bases in Lithuania and Romania, gathering reconnaissance on Russian military positions and movements.
item.dc:creator: New York Times

item.title: 🇮🇷🛢️ OPEC Oil Output at Lowest Since 2000 as Iran Faces Blockade
item.description: OPEC oil production has fallen to its lowest level since at least 2000 as the US blockade squeezes Iranian exports. The production decline underscores the impact of the US-Iran conflict on global energy markets.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧💼 UK Faces Pressure Over Foreign Proxy Operations
item.description: The UK is cracking down on foreign proxies acting on behalf of Russia, Iran, and China, but loopholes in the system may allow some networks to continue operations. Security officials warn of increasing threats from state-sponsored actors.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 Taiwan Tests HIMARS Rocket Systems in Defense Drill
item.description: Taiwan's military tested US-supplied HIMARS rocket systems as part of defense exercises amid rising tensions with China. The drills demonstrate Taiwan's efforts to boost its defensive capabilities against potential Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇬🇧🏛️ UK Faces Parliamentary Pressure Over Defense Spending
item.description: British lawmakers are pressing the government to increase defense spending amid rising global threats. The debate comes as NATO allies face pressure to meet spending commitments and counter growing security challenges.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸⚔️ US Military Operations Against Iran Continue
item.description: US Central Command forces are conducting self-defense strikes against Iran after Tehran shot down a US Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. The operation marks a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: CBS News

item.title: 🇺🇳🍞 World Food Program Warns of Hunger Crisis from Iran War
item.description: The World Food Program has warned that the Iran conflict could trigger a global hunger crisis as oil prices surge and supply chains are disrupted. The organization is calling for urgent international coordination to address food security concerns.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: 🇶🇦🤝 Qatari Negotiators Travel to Tehran for US-Iran Talks
item.description: Qatari mediators have traveled to Tehran in an effort to narrow differences between Washington and Tehran in ongoing negotiations. The diplomatic push comes as both sides exchange military strikes.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🌍🚨 US Allies Condemn Iran's Lethal Plotting in Europe
item.description: US allies across Europe, North America, and Australia have condemned Iran's alleged lethal plotting activities on their territories. The coordinated statement highlights growing international concern over Iranian covert operations.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇾🇪⚔️ Cargo Vessel Exchanges Fire with Armed Craft off Yemen
item.description: A cargo vessel exchanged fire with armed craft off the Yemeni coast, according to the UK Maritime Trade Operations. The incident highlights ongoing security risks in the Red Sea region.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 US Weapons Sales to Taiwan Take Unexpected Turn with Drones
item.description: While the US typically sells weapons to Taiwan, drone technology is seeing reverse flows as Taiwanese innovations attract American interest. The shift reflects Taiwan's growing role in defense technology development.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🇰🇵🇨🇳 North Korea and China Claim Wins, But Limits Remain
item.description: North Korea and China have highlighted gains from Xi Jinping's visit, but analysts note that significant limitations in the bilateral relationship persist. The visit comes amid regional security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇦🇷📉 Argentine Pesos Drops Most in Eight Weeks
item.description: Argentina's peso fell the most in eight weeks amid ongoing economic challenges. The currency pressure reflects persistent inflation concerns and market uncertainty about the country's economic trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg

item.title: 🇺🇦🏛️ Drones Hit Historic Museum in Russian-Annexed Crimea
item.description: Drone strikes damaged a historic museum in Russian-annexed Crimea, with officials altering train schedules in response. The attack adds to the pattern of Ukrainian strikes on Russian-held territory.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇦🇫💥 Pakistan Airstrikes Kill at Least 13 in Afghanistan
item.description: Pakistan conducted airstrikes in Afghanistan that killed at least 13 people, according to Taliban officials. The strikes mark an escalation in cross-border tensions between the neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷💰 Trump Says Iran Must Pay the Price for Delaying Negotiations
item.description: President Donald Trump said Iran has taken too long to negotiate a deal and would have to pay the price after Tehran and Washington exchanged strikes in the region. The warning comes amid continued diplomatic efforts.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
